title: Equity vs debt mutual fund taxation: How your MF gains are taxed in India
summary: Mutual fund taxation in India varies by fund type and holding period. Equity funds face different tax rates for short-term and long-term gains, while debt funds purchased after April 1, 2023, are taxed at income tax slab rates. Details here.

title: Sweep-in fixed deposit facility: A smart way to partially withdraw from FD and earn higher returns
summary: A sweep-in facility is a feature that helps you earn better returns on excess funds lying idle in your savings or current account. Under this facility, any sum in your savings account above a pre-set limit is automatically transferred into an FD.

title: ITR: Here's why it is important to ensure TDS statement, Form 26AS details match before you file income-tax returns
summary: It is important to ensure that details on your TDS statements (Form 16 and 16A) and financial statement Form 26AS match before you complete your income-tax returns so as to ensure smooth filing and refund process.

title: Charlie Munger's wealth lessons: 5 habits to give up for long-term success in life and investing

title: I asked ChatGPT the actual cost of a child's school education in Delhi: AI gives me reality-check
summary: In Delhi/NCR, private school fees range from ₹1.5 lakh to over ₹30 lakh annually, depending on the school's tier. Parents often overspend on elite schooling while neglecting emotional stability and family involvement, which are crucial for children's development.

title: 8th pay commission: Meeting with employee representatives scheduled in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh — Check dates
summary: The 8th Pay Commission will visit Lucknow on June 22-23 to gather input on salary structures from stakeholders. Chaired by former SC Justice Ranjana Prakash Desai, the commission will hold consultations across India.

title: How are profits from intraday and Futures & Options trading taxed in India? Explained
summary: In India, intraday trading profits are taxed as speculative business income, while Futures &amp; Options trading profits are non-speculative. Here are the income tax rules.
